features: The recipe page now has a "Used in recipes" block — it shows which recipes use this component directly as an ingredient, with quick links to open them
Handy when you change a preparation (cream, dough, sauce) and want to see at a glance which dishes it's used in
Recipes can now be organized into folders. The recipes page has a new view-mode switch: "Groupings" (the previous default) and "Folders" — a navigable hierarchy with breadcrumbs.
Folders support a custom name, icon, and color. Use the menu on a folder tile to edit or delete it.
Selected recipes can be moved into a folder via the "Move to..." action.
Added shrinkage tracking (mass loss during cooking). On the recipe page, you can specify a percentage or final weight — the cost will be recalculated taking the loss into account.
Informational blocks with explanations of the calculations have been added to the calculation page.
Price changes can be tracked in products. If a product has a price set, a price chart button will be available in the unit cost field.

When creating a recipe, you can now create products on the fly. Click +, enter the name of a product you don't yet have in stock, and select a unit of measurement. After saving the recipe, the product will be created automatically; you just need to enter the correct price for further calculations.

The ability to share a recipe link has been added. Currently, there are limitations—recipes with nested recipes cannot be shared. When clicking the generated link, the user will be able to save the recipe in their app.
Additionally, you can export a specific recipe directly from the recipe page and share it as a file.
